blocks.text: Refer to: A Plus Home Health

Printed By: Mayra Reyes, FRONT DESK ASSISTANT 2/11/2026 8:22:17 AM
The information on this page is CONFIDENTIAL Any release of this information requires
the expressed written authorization of the patient listed above.
Patient is here to follow up on her neuropathy.
01/26/26- Patient was last seen in office 09/23/2024 by Venkat Veerappan for neuropathy. She
is following up today on her neuropathy. She said that she wants to talk about what can be
recommended for her nerves. She said that she notices on her right side and it pinches in her
lower back and she doesn't want to have surgery. She said that shes trying PT and exercise.
She said that she has noticed that the nerves on the right side hands and feet aren't great. She
said that she doesn't like medications. She doesn't like the blood pressure machine and it gives
her anxiety. She said that she runs 4 times a week in cali and its 4.82 miles and that she does
Pt is interested in getting medicine for her numbness/tingling. She has been staying active, she
runs, does pilates, and yoga. Dr. Duke wants for her to have surgery however she is trying to
Pt complains of tingling in the sole of her right foot. This is intermittent in nature. Pt also
mentions that her hands sometimes when she is doing weights her fingers get stuck. After
doing some massages it goes away. This all started around last year however it has been
worsening recently. Pt denies experiencing any weakness on her hands, denies dropping any
objects. Pt denies any numbness/tingling anywhere else. Pt also denies any neck pain.
Tingliness is not severe enough for her to want to take medicine.
Low back pain is exacerbated when she is resting. Pain radiates down her RLE. This is
Endorses low back pain and right buttuck pain
Endorses numbness and tingling in the RLE
Denies any falls or gait disturbances
Father deceased at age 102, no medical issues
Printed By: Mayra Reyes, FRONT DESK ASSISTANT 2/11/2026 8:22:26 AM

5) magnesium, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily
Wt: 90.6 lb Ht/Ln: 62 in BMI: 16.6 BP: 134/88 Pulse: 84 Temp: 98.0F
Pt is awake, alert & oriented X3. NAD.
Speech, Cognition, Mood & Affect are Within Normal Limits.
Sense of smell, taste & hearing to fingers rubbing is intact.
Head/Neck rotation and shoulder shrugging is intact B/L & symmetrical.
Motor: Bulk & tone normal throughout. Power is 5/5 & symmetrical in both upper & lower
Reflexes: Deep Tendon Reflexes are 3/4 at biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, patellae & achilles.
Sensory: Light touch, pinprick, cold, position & vibration sensation is intact throughout &
Cerebellar: Finger-nose-finger & Heel-knee-shin exams are normal. Rapid alternating
Gait: Casual gait is normal. Tandem gait is normal. Heel & toe walking is normal.
Extremities: Hands: L index finger: heberdens nodule. R foot: hallux valgus, dorsal muscle
atrophy. L foot: hallux valgus, dorsal muscle atrophy, hammer toes digits 2-5.
# Peripheral neuropathy (G64): B/L UE and LE
Plan printed and provided to patient:
ADDED Current Meds: magnesium, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily, # 0, RF: 0.
Dr.Anthony Ricciardi - Foot Surgeon - surgery was planned for Jan 2025 however she refused
On Celebrex 100mg po daily prn from PCP
Refer to occupational therapy for hand fingers stiffness (fingers getting stuck) and lumbar
Will repeat EMG/NCS of BUE to see if there is more nerve damage
